analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet argues that China has the right to present facts and defend its sovereignty, security, development interests, and national dignity against slander and maligning by others. Evaluation: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) – Implicitly aligns: the tweet claims "others" are slandering and maligning China, casting China as a victim of hostile attacks, which fits CH1's depiction of external hostility (often the West). CH2 (China is a benevolent power) – Not supported; no emphasis on cooperation, multilateralism, or global public goods. CH3 (China has an epic history) – Not referenced. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values) – Partially adjacent but not directly promoted; the tweet focuses on self-defense rights and dignity, not on the virtues of China’s political system or domestic values. CH5 (CCP government succeeds) – Not mentioned. CH6 (culture/nature) – Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes CH1.
